messages.text: The clamoring echo throws a note the world played an age ago back at you one more time, and another, and another, each repeat harder than the last, hammering the same struck sound into you until your skull rings with it. The blow {verb} you!

The clamoring echo flings its battering repeat toward you and the same old note comes around again to land — and you step through the gap between two reflections where the sound thins to nothing, and it crashes into the wall behind you.

The clamoring echo folds every repeat of the whole worn-out note onto you at once, an age of a sound played back and back and back arriving in a single crash, and for one ringing instant your own heartbeat is drowned under the battering of it! The blow {verb} you!
